mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp increase in the unemployment rate above 10% signals deteriorating labor market conditions, prompting risk-off sentiment and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Strong housing starts (HOUST) combined with declining consumer prices (CPIAUCSL) indicate a balanced macro environment where equity valuations stabilize, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC drops below 0.05 over any 3-year window or 60d returns in co-moving HOUST/CPIAUCSL regimes are negative.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) during periods of monetary tightening (FEDFUNDS > 4.5%) signals resilient corporate earnings, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in high-FEDFUNDS/INDPRO-up regimes are negative or Rank IC < 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll gains (PAYEMS) exceeding 100k in a month indicate robust labor market dynamics, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in high-PAYEMS regimes are negative or Rank IC < 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in real sales (RSAFS) during rising inflation (CPIAUCSL > 3% YoY) indicates stagflationary pressures, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns in high-CPIAUCSL/RSAFS-down regimes are positive or Rank IC > 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in money supply (RSAFS) combined with rising Treasury yields (FEDFUNDS) signals tightening financial conditions, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns in co-moving RSAFS/FEDFUNDS regimes are positive or Rank IC < 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production (INDPRO) during low inflation (CPIAUCSL < 2% YoY) indicates strong real economic growth, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns in high-INDPRO/low-CPIAUCSL regimes are negative or Rank IC < 0.03.
